sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Stephanie Morales, is a 44-year-old female who presents 6 weeks post-right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty.
She reports numbness on the right side, but states that the knee is treating well and that she has no other problems.
She mentions still experiencing numbness on the right side, which the doctor explains will improve as the nerves heal.
She is happy with the surgery and has no pain.
She also mentions that she works for UPS and is unable to return to work due to the physical demands of the job.
Past medical history:
The patient had right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty 6 weeks ago.
Review of systems:
She mentions numbness on the right side, but does not report any other symptoms.
Current medications:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The patient's gait was antalgic with a slight limp and decreased velocity, but she is not using an assistive device.
The right knee focused exam has a healed surgical scar, mild swelling, tenderness along the area of the incision, and range of motion is 0 to 120 degrees.
Everything else is normal.
Test Results:
Radiographs of the right knee show implants in a stable position with no evidence of loosening or early complications.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is 6 weeks post-right medial unicompartmental arthroplasty with a stable implant and no evidence of complications.
Problem list: Right knee arthroplasty; Numbness on the right side; Unable to return to work due to physical demands of the job; Status: active
Plan:
I prescribed no medications.
I will have my nurse Anna give the patient a note to excuse her from work for another 6 weeks.
I will see the patient again in 6 weeks for a telemedicine visit to check in and make sure things are progressing okay.
I advised the patient to continue with physical therapy to work on strength, range of motion, and endurance.
Follow-up:
The patient will return in 6 weeks for a telemedicine visit.
